informal: Show that any prime divisor of $x^{4}-x^{2}+1$ is congruent to 1 modulo 12 .
reasoning: The informal statement says: every prime p dividing x^4 - x^2 + 1 (for some integer x) satisfies p ≡ 1 (mod 12). The Lean signature universally quantifies over a prime p and an integer x, assumes p ∣ (x^4 - x^2 + 1), and concludes (p : ℤ) ≡ 1 [ZMOD 12]. This matches the informal claim exactly: same hypotheses (p prime, x integer, divisibility), same conclusion (congruence mod 12). The use of ℤ for x and the ZMOD congruence are standard equivalent encodings. No extra hypotheses (like p odd, p > 3) are added, and none are dropped. Hence the Lean statement is logically equivalent to the informal one.
